- **Method seed (`--from-method`) — shipped, reduced scope.** Normative codes: **SEED-10** (classification; non-controller constants), **SEED-25**, and **SEED-22** (viability-gate policy). The resolution gate passed at **82.1% ≥ 70%**; the test-candidate precision gate failed at **0.6996 < 0.70**, so the shipped recipe includes the method primary, same-file expansion, and constants—but no test-candidate leg. Evidence: [specs/seeds.md](specs/seeds.md), [method RESULTS](eval/seed-spikes/method/RESULTS.md), and [PROJECT_TRACKER.md](PROJECT_TRACKER.md).

  Nothing blocks further work on the shipped method scope. Re-promoting its test leg requires a **new pre-registered spike** using a better-than-token matching rule, then passing its gate; this is explicitly required by SEED-25. Any compiler-behavior change also requires the Tier 0 corpus re-scan under SEED-23.

- **Route seed (`--from-route`) — not shipped.** Normative requirement: **CLI-17c** governs route-shaped input as suggest-only coaching; the seed matrix records the route proposal’s **Phase 5c double gate** under **SEED-22** (resolution ≥70% plus Front-B margin ≥+10 points). Resolution failed at **0.243 < 0.70**; the margin gate passed but was moot. Therefore route evidence remains coaching-only and no route resolver shipped. Evidence: [specs/seeds.md](specs/seeds.md), [CLI-17c](specs/cli.md), [route RESULTS](eval/seed-spikes/route/RESULTS.md), and [README.md](README.md).

  Before route work can ship, it must be reopened with a **new frozen pre-registration** and a resolver likely honoring router order/constraints, scored on verb-qualified evidence; both gates must then pass. No implementation pass is currently authorized by the recorded status.

final_answer:
- **Method seed — shipped, constrained.** Normative requirements: `SEED-4`, `SEED-5`, `SEED-7`, `SEED-10`, `SEED-22`, and especially `SEED-25`; CLI exposure is specified by `CLI-5`/the `--from-method` table. The resolution gate passed at **82.1% ≥ 70%**, so `--from-method` shipped with exact constant resolution, the method primary, same-file BFS, and constant scanning. Its test-candidate leg failed at **0.6996 < 0.70**, so it was deliberately excluded. Evidence: [`specs/seeds.md`](specs/seeds.md), [`specs/cli.md`](specs/cli.md), [`eval/seed-spikes/method/RESULTS.md`](eval/seed-spikes/method/RESULTS.md).

  Before additional method behavior can ship, a **new pre-registered viability spike** is required for any attempted test-leg re-promotion, using a better-than-token matching rule. Any compiler change also requires the Tier 0 corpus re-scan under `SEED-23`, plus the normal full test suite and fixture-eval update where behavior changes.

- **Route seed — not shipped.** Normative requirements: `SEED-4`, `SEED-5`, `SEED-7`, `SEED-22`, `SEED-18`, and `CLI-17c` (coaching-only route guidance). The Phase 5c double gate decided status: resolution failed at **0.243 < 0.70**; the Front B margin passed (**+0.124 ≥ +0.10**) but is moot because both gates were required. Therefore route evidence remains suggest-only/coaching-only; there is no shipped `--from-route` compiler path. Evidence: [`specs/seeds.md`](specs/seeds.md), [`specs/cli.md`](specs/cli.md), [`eval/seed-spikes/route/RESULTS.md`](eval/seed-spikes/route/RESULTS.md).

  Before route work can ship, it must have a **new frozen pre-registration and spike**, likely testing router-order-faithful, verb-qualified resolution; both resolution and Front B gates must pass, followed by implementation, Tier 0 re-scan, tests, and fixture evaluations.

final_answer:
An explicit files seed flows as follows:

1. **Public API / CLI input.** Ruby callers use `Ctxpack::Seed.files(paths)` and pass the seed to `Ctxpack.compile`; the public seam also accepts an injectable history provider (`lib/ctxpack.rb:13-21`). CLI callers use `--from-files PATH…`; parsing collects repeated/comma-separated paths, validates existence, and constructs the seed (`lib/ctxpack/cli.rb:290-319`, `366-373`). Existing non-test path positional sugar is also classified as a files seed (`lib/ctxpack/cli.rb:343-355`). This implements `SEED-2`, `SEED-10`, `SEED-12`, and `CLI-1`.

2. **Normalization.** `Seed.files` requires at least one path, then canonicalizes paths relative to the application root: absolute/drive paths and root escapes are rejected, separators become `/`, and duplicates collapse (`lib/ctxpack/seed.rb:28-60`). Evidence is stored in caller order as newline-separated paths; identity derives deterministically from the first path (`lib/ctxpack/seed.rb:33-38`). This is `SEED-8` and `SEED-9`: canonicalization precedes identity, storage, deduplication, ordering, and source selection.

3. **Compilation and merge.** `Compiler#compile` normalizes file seeds again, resolves each seed, merges multi-seed packets, then enriches history (`lib/ctxpack/compiler.rb:40-45`, `136-155`). The files recipe verifies every primary, adds each with `files_seed_primary`, then adds only deterministic neighbors—controller tests/specs, same-prefix views, and conventional constant neighbors—using `files_seed_neighbor` (`lib/ctxpack/compiler.rb:885-943`, `946-970`). Multi-seed merging unions normalized paths, merges evidence/reason codes, and applies global budgets once (`lib/ctxpack/compiler.rb:61-106`). Governing codes: `SEED-15`, `MERGE-2`–`MERGE-4`, `FMT-6`.

4. **Optional history.** After final retention, the first `files_seed_primary` is selected. At most one provider call receives app root, repository root, canonical path, and full commit (`lib/ctxpack/compiler.rb:111-134`). The adapter invokes `git-recon facts --format=json --at FULL_SHA -- REPO_PATH`, bounded by one call, five facts, 2,048-byte payload, 16,384-byte response, and 20 seconds (`lib/ctxpack/git_recon_history_provider.rb:152-169`; `Compiler::LIMITS`, `lib/ctxpack/compiler.rb:10-20`). Failure preserves primary evidence and emits typed omission/follow-up. This is `SEED-27`, `FMT-13`, and `LIM-5`.

5. **Packet/rendering.** The packet is the shared contract (`lib/ctxpack/packet.rb:97-129`). Markdown renders seeds, inspect-first inventory, evidence, optional history, run commands, and follow-ups; manifest rendering serializes the same packet object (`lib/ctxpack/markdown_renderer.rb:10-23`, `lib/ctxpack/manifest_renderer.rb:9-11`). Files packets emit format v4 and `files_seed_*` provenance (`FMT-2`, `FMT-6`, `FMT-13`, `MAN-1`).

6. **Regression surface.** `test/fixtures/evals/files_seed_accounts_controller.yml` asserts primary/neighbor inclusion, exclusions, test command, and `max_files`; `FixtureEvalsTest` runs packet assertions, limit checks, and two-run Markdown/manifest hash determinism (`test/ctxpack/fixture_evals_test.rb:28-94`, `132-145`). History behavior is covered by `test/ctxpack/history_integration_test.rb:21-58`.

Determinism is `DET-1`/`DET-2`: same repo state, task, normalized seeds, and history result yield the same normalized packet. Core limits are eight total files, four constants, two views, two tests, and 120 snippet lines per file (`Compiler::LIMITS`).
